title: FIR versus IIR filter tradeoffs
description: FIR filters are always stable with linear phase but need many taps, while IIR filters reach sharp cutoffs at low order with nonlinear phase.
body: Use when choosing a filter family for a constrained target. Do not assume IIR is always cheaper, because its feedback complicates fixed-point stability and parallelization. Actions: compare order against attenuation for both families, inspect the phase response, and record which constraint dominates. Validation signals include an FIR group-delay flatness check and an IIR pole-location test confirming stability margins. Risk boundary: IIR feedback can amplify quantization into limit cycles, so escalate to FIR or higher precision when limit cycles appear in fixed point.
